skill.name: docker-expert
skill.description: Provides Docker guidance for Dockerfile optimization, multi-stage builds, image size reduction, container security, Docker Compose, and production deployment patterns.
skill.summary: Improve Docker builds, Compose setups, and container security with practical review guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical heuristic because Docker command examples, localhost health checks, filesystem paths, and secret-management examples appear together in one instructional skill. Review found no prompt injection, hidden executable file, credential exfiltration, or malicious network target. The remaining risk is medium because the skill explicitly guides agents to run Docker CLI commands that can inspect, build, start, stop, and push containers when a user allows tool execution.

medium_findings.title: Docker CLI Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for operational risk: the skill recommends shell fallback commands that inspect Docker state, build images, run containers, stop containers, and push multi-architecture images. This is legitimate for a Docker expert skill, but it can mutate the local Docker environment and should require normal tool approval and workspace review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are shown directly in fenced bash blocks and include docker build, docker run, docker stop, and docker buildx build --push. Confidence is high for operational risk, but no malicious intent or hidden execution path is present.

medium_findings.title: Build-Time Secret Example Requires Careful Use
medium_findings.description: NEEDS REVIEW: the BuildKit example reads a secret from /run/secrets/api_key into an API_KEY shell variable during a build step. The surrounding section presents secrets management guidance, not exfiltration, but users could leak secrets into image layers if they adapt the pattern incorrectly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code is an instructional Dockerfile snippet rather than executable marketplace code. The risk depends on user adaptation, so the concern is moderate and context-dependent.

low_findings.title: Fenced Docker and Compose Snippets Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: most external command alerts occur inside Markdown examples for Dockerfile, Compose, and validation workflows. They are visible instructional snippets for the declared Docker domain and are not hidden scripts that execute when the skill loads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected patterns are fenced examples and align with the skill purpose. No evidence found that the skill executes these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: Localhost Health Checks Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: the hardcoded URLs are localhost health-check examples for Dockerfile and Compose health probes. They do not target an external domain and do not transmit credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs point to http://localhost:3000/health inside documented health check examples. No evidence found of outbound data transfer.

low_findings.title: Secret Names Flagged as Credential Access
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: POSTGRES_PASSWORD_FILE, db_password, and api_key appear in examples explaining Docker secrets. The skill recommends avoiding environment-variable secrets and using Docker-managed secret files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is secrets-management documentation, not credential harvesting. No evidence found of network transmission or logging of these values.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Paths Are Docker Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: /dev/null redirects, /root/.npm cache mounts, /run/secrets paths, and /usr/local/bin examples are normal Docker documentation patterns. They are not broad filesystem traversal or hidden file collection logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths appear in visible command examples and Dockerfile snippets. No evidence found of code that enumerates sensitive host files.

low_findings.title: Keyword-Based Cryptography and Reconnaissance Flags
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: weak-cryptography and reconnaissance alerts appear to be keyword matches in ordinary prose such as description text, checklist items, and routing guidance. No cryptographic operation or reconnaissance workflow is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation text, not runnable crypto or scanning code. No evidence found of weak algorithm use or network reconnaissance instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Approved Docker Command Blocks
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell examples for Docker environment detection, build validation, runtime validation, and multi-platform image publishing. These patterns are expected for Docker support but should run only after user approval because they can change local Docker state or publish an image tag.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and domain-relevant. The main uncertainty is whether a host tool policy will require approval before executing the commands.

content.user_title: Optimize Docker Builds and Container Security
content.value_statement: Docker projects often suffer from slow builds, large images, and unsafe runtime defaults. This skill reviews Dockerfiles and Compose files with practical guidance for optimization, security, and production readiness.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Dockerfiles for layer caching, base image choice, multi-stage structure, and production artifact handling.
Suggests Docker Compose patterns for health checks, dependencies, networks, volumes, and resource limits.
Identifies container security improvements such as non-root users, secret handling, and reduced attack surface.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for slow builds, large images, networking issues, and development container workflows.
Offers validation commands for Docker builds, runtime checks, Compose configuration, and image inspection.
limitations: Does not provide deep Kubernetes orchestration support beyond recommending a specialist.
Requires access to project Dockerfiles or Compose files for precise recommendations.
May suggest Docker CLI commands that need user approval before execution.
Does not replace vulnerability scanners or environment-specific compliance review.

use_cases.title: Reduce Production Image Size
use_cases.description: Refactor Dockerfiles into multi-stage builds and copy only runtime artifacts into the final image.

use_cases.title: Harden Container Runtime Defaults
use_cases.description: Review images for non-root execution, secret handling, health checks, and reduced package footprint.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Compose-Based Development
use_cases.description: Improve service dependencies, networks, volumes, debug ports, and health checks for local teams.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Dockerfile
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Dockerfile for build speed, image size, and basic security. Explain the highest-impact fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Docker Compose
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my Compose file for service dependencies, health checks, networks, volumes, and resource limits. Suggest production-safe changes.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Multi-Stage Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this Dockerfile into an optimized multi-stage build. Preserve runtime behavior and explain each stage.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Container Security
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these Docker and Compose files for container security risks, secret exposure, root execution, and runtime hardening gaps.

output_examples.input: A Dockerfile copies the full source tree before installing dependencies.
output: Move dependency manifests before source copy to improve layer caching.
Install production dependencies separately from build dependencies.
Add a focused ignore file to reduce build context size.

output_examples.input: A Compose file starts an app and database without health checks.
output: Add a database health check before the app starts.
Place the database on an internal backend network.
Define named volumes for persistent database data.

output_examples.input: A production container runs as root and stores secrets in environment variables.
output: Create a non-root application user with a stable UID.
Use Docker secrets or mounted secret files for sensitive values.
Limit runtime capabilities and keep the image minimal.

best_practices: Share the relevant Dockerfile, Compose file, and build goal before asking for changes.
Review suggested Docker commands before approving execution in a local environment.
Validate final recommendations with a build, runtime smoke test, and security scan.
anti_patterns: Do not copy the full source tree before dependency installation when layer caching matters.
Do not store production secrets in image layers or plain environment variables.
Do not run production containers as root unless a documented exception exists.

faq.question: Can this skill rewrite a Dockerfile?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose Dockerfile changes for caching, multi-stage builds, image size, and runtime security.

faq.question: Does it support Docker Compose?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers service dependencies, health checks, networks, volumes, resource limits, and environment separation.

faq.question: Will it run Docker commands automatically?
faq.answer: It may suggest Docker CLI commands. Execution depends on the host tool policy and user approval.

faq.question: Can it help with Kubernetes?
faq.answer: Only at a basic boundary level. The skill recommends a Kubernetes specialist for orchestration details.

faq.question: Can it find image vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can recommend scanning and hardening steps, but it does not replace a dedicated vulnerability scanner.

faq.question: What files should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide Dockerfiles, Compose files, ignore files, build logs, and deployment constraints for best results.
